title: Debounce IntersectionObserver callbacks
desc: When animating many scroll-reveal elements, batch observer callbacks with requestAnimationFrame instead of triggering style writes per entry.
descAr: عند تحريك عناصر scroll-reveal كثيرة، جمع استدعاءات observer بـ requestAnimationFrame بدلاً من تشغيل كتابات الأنماط لكل عنصر.
summary: IntersectionObserver fires for every element the moment it enters the viewport. With 30+ reveal elements, 30 synchronous style mutations cause layout thrash and dropped frames. Batch them into a single rAF flush.
problem:
IntersectionObserver fires for each element as soon as it enters the viewport. With 30+ reveal elements on a page, triggering 30 style mutations synchronously causes layout thrash, dropped frames, and visible jank during scrolling.

The browser recalculates styles after every mutation, but rAF naturally coalesces writes. The fix is collecting entries and flushing once per frame.
solution: Collect observer entries in a Set during the callback, then schedule a single requestAnimationFrame flush. In the rAF callback, iterate the set, apply all class/style changes, then clear the set. This batches all DOM writes into one frame and avoids redundant work. Instead of 30 style recalculations per scroll, you get one.
solutionTitle: Batch entries into a single rAF flush

callout.text: Key idea: the same pattern works for ResizeObserver, MutationObserver, and scroll event listeners. Any callback that fires per-element can be batched with rAF.

baDesc: The fix is wrapping observer logic in a rAF flush, not changing the observer setup itself.

before.desc: 30 individual callbacks, 15ms style recalc per flush.
before.code: // Bad: style mutations per entry observer = new IntersectionObserver(entries => { entries.forEach(en => { en.target.classList.add('visible'); // 30 separate style recalculations! }); });

after.desc: One style recalc per scroll frame (~0.5ms).
after.code: // Good: batch into rAF const pending = new Set(); observer = new IntersectionObserver(entries => { entries.forEach(en => pending.add(en.target)); requestAnimationFrame(() => { pending.forEach(el => el.classList.add('visible')); pending.clear(); }); });
